evals.prompt:
Stripe integration round prep assist: based on interview-research skill i have the following questions compiled:

1. The Invoicing Service (Consuming External APIs) - 3 parts: file parsing, API pagination with next_page_token, business logic with deduplication by event_id
2. Payment Gateway Proxy - fault tolerance: wrapper class, timeout + status check, circuit breaker
3. Subscription Tracker - map wrapper, get_active_users O(1), command string parser

Let's start with the Invoicing Service.
evals.expected_output: Should give a structured breakdown of the Invoicing Service question (restate, inputs/outputs, edge cases), classify it as Local/integration, and generate Maven project scaffolding with pom.xml, skeleton class with Part 1/2/3 TODOs, and sample customer_ids.json. Should NOT write the implementation.

assertions.name: does_not_give_complete_solution
assertions.description: Response must not contain a working fetchAllPages() or full InvoicingService implementation with real logic filled in

assertions.name: generates_pom_xml
assertions.description: Response includes a pom.xml with Java 21, OkHttp, and Gson dependencies

assertions.name: generates_skeleton_class
assertions.description: Response includes a Java skeleton class with TODOs marking Part 1, Part 2, Part 3

assertions.name: creates_sample_input_file
assertions.description: Response includes a sample customer_ids.json or similar input resource file

assertions.name: asks_for_candidate_plan
assertions.description: Response ends with a question asking the candidate what their plan is for Part 1, not just dumping the scaffold

evals.prompt: ok so i've read the invoicing service question. for Part 1 i think i'll just use a Scanner to read the file, then call the API in a for loop. my plan is to make the HTTP call inside the loop and print the result each time.
evals.expected_output: Should not correct them harshly. Should acknowledge the Scanner idea but nudge toward Files.readAllLines / InputStream since the question flags it as a failure point. Should ask about the JSON parsing approach before they write code. Should NOT rewrite their plan or give the implementation.

assertions.name: mentions_files_api_or_inputstream
assertions.description: Response mentions Files.readAllLines, Files.readString, or InputStream as a more reliable alternative, since the question explicitly flags Scanner as a common failure point

assertions.name: does_not_write_implementation
assertions.description: Response does not write a complete readFile() or main() method

assertions.name: asks_followup_question
assertions.description: Response asks at least one follow-up question about their approach (e.g., how will you parse the JSON? what format is the file?)

evals.expected_output: Should review the code using the structured format (Correctness, Pattern fit, One thing to tighten, What's solid). Should flag: response.body() can be null (NPE risk), no timeout on the OkHttpClient, no retry on failure. Should NOT rewrite the method. Should note what's solid (pagination loop structure is correct).

assertions.name: uses_review_format
assertions.description: Response uses the structured review format with Correctness, Pattern fit, One thing to tighten, What's solid sections

assertions.name: flags_null_body
assertions.description: Response flags the response.body() null risk (NPE)

assertions.name: flags_missing_timeout
assertions.description: Response mentions missing timeout on the HTTP client

assertions.name: does_not_rewrite_method
assertions.description: Response does not produce a fully corrected fetchAllUsage() method — it describes issues and lets the candidate fix them

assertions.name: acknowledges_correct_pagination_pattern
assertions.description: Response notes that the do-while pagination loop structure is correct
